---
title: "Dataset: CTU-13 botnet netflow (Stratosphere Lab)"
description: "A labelled botnet network-flow dataset ŌĆö included as a scaffold pending an explicit scope keep/cut decision, because it sits at the network layer the project deliberately excludes."
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
---

::: {.callout-warning title="SCOPE DECISION UNRESOLVED ŌĆö do not publish without resolving"}
This page documents the dataset fully, but the dataset's place in the project is **not settled**. CTU-13 is network-layer botnet command-and-control netflow, which falls under the project's explicit out-of-scope exclusions (network intrusion, malware, network-level botnets). It is documented so the keep/cut decision can be made against a concrete page, **not** so it ships by default. Resolve the scope question (live-site TODO ┬¦9) before this leaves draft. The lean in that note is *cut*.
:::

::: {.lead}
Thirteen separate Argus bidirectional netflow captures (~20 million flows total), each a different botnet family, with a free-text `Label` annotation coarsenable into Botnet / Legitimate / Background classes. Real IP/port network metadata from a controlled university lab capture. Genuinely labelled and technically a "botnet" dataset ŌĆö but at the network flow layer, not the web-facing application layer the project is about.
:::

## Why this is a scope question, not a straightforward fit

The project's subject is automated abuse against **web-facing flows** ŌĆö sessions, requests, application-layer behaviour. CTU-13 is **network-layer** data: bidirectional flow records (source/destination IP, ports, protocol, byte/packet counts, Argus connection state). That is the territory of intrusion detection and SOC tooling, which the project's scope documents exclude by name. Three further properties weaken the fit even on its own terms:

- The `Background` class (96% of flows) is unlabelled real traffic from the same network ŌĆö **not** verified-legitimate, so it isn't a clean negative class.
- The 13 scenarios are different botnet families captured at different times ŌĆö not one time series, and concatenating them mixes captures.
- The actor unit is a **host (IP)**, not a user or session; there is no application-layer or user-level identifier.

It has "botnet" in its lineage, which is exactly the temptation: the label says botnet, but the layer is wrong. The honest call is whether network-flow botnet C2 belongs in a web-facing-abuse project at all.

## Access

Source: CTU-13 dataset, Czech Technical University (Stratosphere Lab) ŌĆö `https://mcfp.felk.cvut.cz/publicDatasets/CTU-13-Dataset/`.

- Local path: `data/CTU-13-Dataset`
- File format: per-scenario `.binetflow` CSV (Argus bidirectional NetFlow export).
- Date inspected: 2026-05-23.
- 13 scenario files on disk, ranging from 13.9 MB (scenario 11) to 610.0 MB (scenario 3).

## Structure

- 13 scenarios; each a separate Argus bidirectional netflow capture. Record granularity: one bidirectional flow per row.
- Total rows (all scenarios concatenated): 19,976,700; columns: 16 (14 native + a `scenario` tag added by the loader).
- Time range across scenarios (`StartTime` as string): 2011/08/10 09:46:53 to 2011/08/19 11:45:43.
- No join keys between scenarios; each capture is independent. Within a scenario, flows link by `SrcAddr`/`DstAddr`/port tuples.

Per-scenario row counts:

| scenario | file | rows | size |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `capture20110810.binetflow` | 2,824,636 | 368.7 MB |
| 2 | `capture20110811.binetflow` | 1,808,122 | 235.8 MB |
| 3 | `capture20110812.binetflow` | 4,710,638 | 610.0 MB |
| 4 | `capture20110815.binetflow` | 1,121,076 | 146.7 MB |
| 5 | `capture20110815-2.binetflow` | 129,832 | 16.9 MB |
| 6 | `capture20110816.binetflow` | 558,919 | 73.2 MB |
| 7 | `capture20110816-2.binetflow` | 114,077 | 14.9 MB |
| 8 | `capture20110816-3.binetflow` | 2,954,230 | 385.2 MB |
| 9 | `capture20110817.binetflow` | 2,087,508 | 272.6 MB |
| 10 | `capture20110818.binetflow` | 1,309,791 | 170.8 MB |
| 11 | `capture20110818-2.binetflow` | 107,251 | 13.9 MB |
| 12 | `capture20110819.binetflow` | 325,471 | 42.6 MB |
| 13 | `capture20110815-3.binetflow` | 1,925,149 | 250.5 MB |

## Schema

All columns are real (non-anonymised) network metadata; IPs are real but from a controlled lab capture.

| column | dtype | example | description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `StartTime` | String | `2011/08/10 09:46:59.607825` | Flow start timestamp (microsecond precision) |
| `Dur` | Float64 | `1.026539` | Flow duration in seconds |
| `Proto` | String | `tcp` | L4 protocol (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) |
| `SrcAddr` | String | `94.44.127.113` | Source IP address |
| `Sport` | String | `1577` | Source port |
| `Dir` | String | `   ->` | Flow direction marker |
| `DstAddr` | String | `147.32.84.59` | Destination IP address |
| `Dport` | String | `6881` | Destination port |
| `State` | String | `S_RA` | Argus flow state (connection state code) |
| `sTos` | Int64 | `0` | Source ToS byte |
| `dTos` | Int64 | `0` | Destination ToS byte |
| `TotPkts` | Int64 | `4` | Total packets in flow |
| `TotBytes` | Int64 | `276` | Total bytes in flow |
| `SrcBytes` | Int64 | `156` | Bytes sent by source |
| `Label` | String | `flow=Background-Established-cm` | Argus label string (`Background`/`From-Botnet`/`To-Botnet`/`LEGITIMATE`) |
| `scenario` | String | `1` | Scenario folder id (1..13) ŌĆö added by loader |

## Label

Label column: `Label` (free-text Argus annotation), coarsened by string match into Botnet / Legitimate / Background / Other.

| label_class | count | rate |
|---|---|---|
| Background | 19,175,568 | 0.95990 |
| Botnet | 444,699 | 0.02226 |
| Other | 356,433 | 0.01784 |

Distinct raw label strings across all scenarios: 1,400. Botnet traffic is heavily outnumbered by background. **Background flows are unlabelled real traffic and must not be treated as confirmed-legitimate** ŌĆö the absence of a clean negative class is a real limitation for any supervised use.

## Identifier inventory

Identifiers are real IP addresses and ports ŌĆö no user IDs, no device fingerprints. Within a scenario, infected hosts have known IPs (documented in each scenario `README`). The actor unit is the host (IP); there is no user-level identifier.

| column | n_unique | null_rate | role |
|---|---|---|---|
| `SrcAddr` | 2,031,491 | 0.0000 | source IP ŌĆö strongest actor identifier (per-host) |
| `DstAddr` | 531,964 | 0.0000 | destination IP ŌĆö contacted endpoint |
| `Sport` | 118,218 | 0.0102 | source port (often ephemeral) |
| `Dport` | 108,451 | 0.0097 | destination port (service identifier) |
| `Proto` | 19 | 0.0000 | L4 protocol |

## Temporal structure

- `StartTime`: microsecond-precision string (`YYYY/MM/DD HH:MM:SS.ffffff`).
- `Dur`: float seconds; flow durations span sub-second to hours.
- Each scenario is a contiguous capture window of hours; scenarios are not contemporaneous.

Per-scenario time spans (chronological by date):

| scenario | t_min | t_max | rows |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2011/08/10 09:46:53 | 2011/08/10 15:54:07 | 2,824,636 |
| 2 | 2011/08/11 09:49:35 | 2011/08/11 14:01:11 | 1,808,122 |
| 3 | 2011/08/12 15:24:01 | 2011/08/15 10:13:26 | 4,710,638 |
| 4 | 2011/08/15 10:42:52 | 2011/08/15 15:11:19 | 1,121,076 |
| 5 | 2011/08/15 16:43:20 | 2011/08/15 17:13:26 | 129,832 |
| 13 | 2011/08/15 17:13:40 | 2011/08/16 09:36:00 | 1,925,149 |
| 6 | 2011/08/16 10:01:46 | 2011/08/16 12:10:56 | 558,919 |
| 7 | 2011/08/16 13:51:24 | 2011/08/16 14:12:41 | 114,077 |
| 8 | 2011/08/16 14:18:55 | 2011/08/17 09:47:11 | 2,954,230 |
| 9 | 2011/08/17 11:34:49 | 2011/08/17 17:12:13 | 2,087,508 |
| 10 | 2011/08/18 09:56:29 | 2011/08/18 15:04:59 | 1,309,791 |
| 11 | 2011/08/18 15:39:35 | 2011/08/18 15:55:46 | 107,251 |
| 12 | 2011/08/19 10:02:43 | 2011/08/19 11:45:43 | 325,471 |

## Missing data

Overall null cells: 2,337,061 / 319,627,200 (0.73%). Columns with >20% missingness: none.

Per-column nulls (all scenarios concatenated): `dTos` (0.0860), `sTos` (0.0110), `Sport` (0.0102), `Dport` (0.0097), `State` (0.00007); all other columns 0. `sTos`/`dTos` are commonly null because routers do not always set ToS; port nulls occur for connectionless / ICMP / ARP flows.

## Quirks and observations

- 13 separate captures, each a different botnet family (per scenario `README`) ŌĆö not a single time series.
- `Label` is a free-text Argus annotation, not a clean class column; coarsening requires substring matching (1,400 distinct raw strings).
- `Background` flows are unlabelled real traffic ŌĆö NOT verified-legitimate.
- `Sport`/`Dport` are stored as strings (some non-numeric `0x*` values), not integers.
- IPs are real, from a single university network capture; the actor unit is a host, not a user.
- Concatenating all scenarios is fine for descriptive stats but mixes captures from different dates and botnet families.

## Framing distance

**What real problem it approximates:** network-layer botnet C2 detection from flow records ŌĆö a different problem from web-facing bot abuse, sharing only the word "bot."

**What it fails to represent:** the web/application layer entirely; user- or session-level units; a clean legitimate class; and any single coherent timeline. It represents host-level malware C2 traffic, not automated abuse of web flows.

**What further evidence would be needed:** to be in scope at all, application-layer/session data rather than netflow ŌĆö at which point it would be a different dataset. This is the core of the keep/cut question.

## What it cannot show

Nothing about web-facing bot abuse ŌĆö the project's actual subject. It documents network-flow botnet traffic, which sits outside the project's scope boundary. Its presence here is a decision aid, not an endorsement of inclusion.

## Reproduction

Generated by `notebooks/eda/ctu-13.ipynb`, which calls `openbotrisk.eda.loaders.load_ctu13_meta` (polars read of each `*.binetflow`, then vertical concat).

```bash
jupyter nbconvert --to notebook --execute --inplace \
  notebooks/eda/ctu-13.ipynb \
  --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=600
```

Loader runtime on the reference machine: 1.4 s for all 13 scenarios concatenated in-memory with polars.
